title: 15S24 – Focus – Front Drive Halfshafts Replacement
symptom: quickly, and at no cost to you. You may have already received a letter from Ford regarding Program R1413 to which you have not yet responded. Please note that this letter supersedes the previous letter. If you have recently had Program R1413 completed on your vehicle, no further action is required. It is possible that the front drive shafts fitted to your vehicle were not manufactured according to our stringent quality standards. Shafts that do not meet specification could fail during initial acceleration on take-off. If this occurs it will cause you to lose the ability to accelerate from a standing start. This would render your vehicle immobile and require towing. We want to ensure that you continue to experience trouble-free motoring, so wish to replace your shafts now, free of charge. title: Transmission - Loss Of Motive Power With DTC P0868 - 20 August
symptom: Some 2018-2019 Ranger and Everest vehicles equipped with a 2.0L (Panther) engine and 10R80 transmission may experience loss of motive power while in any gear (forward or reverse) accompanied with Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) P0868. Other DTCs may also be present.

title: Steering Effort, Operation Noise And/or DTC’s C1B00/U3000 - 03 July 2022
symptom: Some 2015-2021 Ranger and Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with EPAS may experience water ingress via punctures in the steering gear boots resulting in increased steering efforts, operational noise and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) such as C1B00 or U3000. In many cases it can be difficult to determine where the boot damage has occurred without applying test methods not usually adopted by dealerships. For this reason, it is recommended to follow this TSB Action on all vehicles that require a replacement EPAS steering gear to be fitted.
concern: Some 2015-2021 Ranger and Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with EPAS may experience water ingress via punctures in the steering gear boots resulting in increased steering efforts, operational noise and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) such as C1B00 or U3000. title: 23P23 - Ranger/Everest Stage V Panther 2.0L Bi-Turbo Charge Air Cooler Duct Loose
symptom: . In some cases, prior to a complete separation (partial separation) hissing noises and oil leak are noticeable. The driver will experience reduction of power, an audible change in engine sound and white smoke from the engine bay. The engine will continue to run at decreased power, but with hesitation and/or a misfire. Dealers are to replace the charge air cooler duct following the attached work procedure. This service must be performed on all affected vehicles at no charge to the vehicle owner.

title: 25S39 S2 – Ranger / Everest 3.0L Lion Engine - Camshaft Sprocket Failure – Final Repair
symptom: For This Safety Recall In all of the affected vehicles, the left-hand camshaft sprocket on the engine may fracture due to a manufacturing defect. This camshaft sprocket failure may result in an immediate loss of motive power (engine stall) with no prior warning to the driver. A loss of motive power without warning increases the risk of a crash.
concern: For This Safety Recall In all of the affected vehicles, the left-hand camshaft sprocket on the engine may fracture due to a manufacturing defect. This camshaft sprocket failure may result in an immediate loss of motive power (engine stall) with no prior warning to the driver. A loss of motive power without warning increases the risk of a crash.

title: 25S82S1 - NEW VEHICLE DEMONSTRATION / DELIVERY HOLD – Final Repair
symptom: For This Safety Recall On some of the affected vehicles, the wheel end hub bolt may become loose and make a clicking noise or break and make a rattle noise. This is often discovered by technicians during routine maintenance such as oil changes and tire rotations and a rattle is found coming from the wheel cap. A broken wheel hub bolt will not result in a wheel separation. However, over time, a broken bolt may lead to wear within the axle and damage to the axle hub splines. Stripped rear axle hub splines can result in vehicle roll away when the vehicle is in park without the parking brake applied or cause a loss of motive power in 4X2 operation. Both of these conditions can increase the risk of a crash. PLEASE NOTE, FOR THIS RECALL ONLY Due to limited global supply and further interruption to parts flowing through to Australia, parts for this recall are further restricted and Ford will in

title: 25SD9 – Certain 2022-2025 Model Year Transit Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV)
symptom: For This Safety Recall On some of the affected vehicles, insufficient insertion depth of the half shaft into the rear electric drive assembly could lead to wearing of the half shaft splines. If the splines are worn such that they do not have sufficient engagement to transfer torque to the axle side-gear, a driver may experience a noticeable seal leak and eventually may experience a loss of motive power. The driver also may experience unintended vehicle movement while the vehicle is in Park if the Electric Parking Brake (EPB) is not applied. Vehicle roll away in Park and/or loss of motive power while driving can increase the risk of a crash.
concern: For This Safety Recall On some of the affected vehicles, insufficient insertion depth of the half shaft into the rear electric drive assembly could lead to wearing of the half shaft splines. If the splines are worn such that they do not have sufficient engagement to transfer torque to the axle side-gear, a driver may experience a noticeable seal leak and eventually may experience a loss of motive power. The driver also may experience unintended vehicle movement while the vehicle is in Park if the Electric Parking Brake (EPB) is not applied. Vehicle roll away in Park and/or loss of motive power while driving can increase the risk of a crash.
description: For This Safety Recall On some of the affected vehicles, insufficient insertion depth of the half shaft into the rear electric drive assembly could lead to wearing of the half shaft splines. title: Engine Overheating And/Or Poor Air Conditioning (A/C) Performance - With DTC P0480 And/Or P0217
symptom: Some 2022-2024 Next-Generation Ranger, Ranger Raptor and Everest vehicles may experience engine overheating and/or poor A/C performance with Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) P0480 and/or P0217. This may be caused by an inoperative cooling fan due to damage to the cooling fan relay and wiring.
concern: Some 2022-2024 Next-Generation Ranger, Ranger Raptor and Everest vehicles may experience engine overheating and/or poor A/C performance with Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) P0480 and/or P0217. This may be caused by an inoperative cooling fan due to damage to the cooling fan relay and wiring.
description: Some 2022-2024 Next-Generation Ranger, Ranger Raptor and Everest vehicles may experience engine overheating and/or poor A/C performance with Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) P0480 and/or P0217. This may be caused by an inoperative cooling fan due to damage to the cooling fan relay and wiring.

title: Transmission Not In Park Message When Driving Below 10kmph - 20 May 2026
symptom: Some of the vehicles listed in the Model statement above may exhibit the following warning messages • <Transmission not in Park> (driver is not detected / driver does not sit in the seat) permanently when driving below 10 km/h (6 mph) either as stand alone message or • In combination with two other messages: <Hill Start Assist Not Available> / <Park Brake Limited Function> or • Stored DTCs P166B in the SCCM sometimes in combination with U0404 in the ABS module, This may be caused by the SOBDMC, PCM, TCM and SCCM module software levels and/or a damaged seat occupant sensor.
concern: Some of the vehicles listed in the Model statement above may exhibit the following warning messages • <Transmission not in Park> (driver is not detected / driver does not sit in the seat) permanently when driving below 10 km/h (6 mph) either as stand alone message or • In combination with two other messages: <Hill Start Assist Not Available> / <Park Brake Limited Function> or • Stored DTCs P166B in the SCCM sometimes in combination with U0404 in the ABS module, This may be caused by the SOBDMC, PCM, TCM and SCCM module software levels and/or a damaged seat occupant sensor.

title: 03 May 2021 - 2011-2021 Ranger
symptom: Some 2011-2021 Ranger and 2015-2021 Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with manual front seat(s) may experience a front seat squeak noise while driving on rough roads and/or with pressure applied to the seat. A possible cause for this concern is due to noisy seat cushion spring(s).

title: B-Pillar Ticking Sound - 25 September
symptom: Some 2022-2024 Ranger double cab and 2022-2024 Everest vehicles may experience a ticking type sound at the B-pillar area.
concern: Some 2022-2024 Ranger double cab and 2022-2024 Everest vehicles may experience a ticking type sound at the B-pillar area.
description: Some 2022-2024 Ranger double cab and 2022-2024 Everest vehicles may experience a ticking type sound at the B-pillar area.

title: Moan/Low Frequency Squealing Noise From The Rear Brakes Under Cold - Weather Conditions While Reversing
symptom: Some 2018-2022 Focus And 2019-2022 Kuga/Escape Vehicles may present with a moan/low frequency squealing noise from the rear brakes while reversing AND braking. The probable cause for this concern is the rear brake system excites the rear suspension system which then emits the low frequency noise.
concern: Some 2018-2022 Focus And 2019-2022 Kuga/Escape Vehicles may present with a moan/low frequency squealing noise from the rear brakes while reversing AND braking. The probable cause for this concern is the rear brake system excites the rear suspension system which then emits the low frequency noise.
description: Some 2018-2022 Focus And 2019-2022 Kuga/Escape Vehicles may present with a moan/low frequency squealing noise from the rear brakes while reversing AND braking. The probable cause for this concern is the rear brake system excites the rear suspension system which then emits the low frequency noise.

title: Rear Lamp - 25 October
symptom: Some 2021-2023 Next-Generation Everest vehicles built to 1-Dec-2022 may experience a water leak from the liftgate into the cabin or water flow from the liftgate drain hole. A possible cause for this concern is a leaking liftgate mounted rear lamp.
concern: Some 2021-2023 Next-Generation Everest vehicles built to 1-Dec-2022 may experience a water leak from the liftgate into the cabin or water flow from the liftgate drain hole. A possible cause for this concern is a leaking liftgate mounted rear lamp.
description: Some 2021-2023 Next-Generation Everest vehicles built to 1-Dec-2022 may experience a water leak from the liftgate into the cabin or water flow from the liftgate drain hole. A possible cause for this concern is a leaking liftgate mounted rear lamp.

title: 2023 - Illuminated MIL With DTCs P066A/C/E, P067A/C/E Or - P06B9/P06BA/BB/BC/BD/BE
symptom: Some 2022-2024 Ranger and Everest vehicles equipped with a 3.0L (Lion) diesel engine built from 20 Jun-2023, may exhibit an illuminated Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) with one or more of the following Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs): P066A/P066C/P066E, P067A/P067C/P067E or P06B9/P06BA/P06BB/P06BC/P06BD/P06BE. • The concern has been isolated and only impacts certain glow plugs. The TSB repair procedure has been revised to only replace the affected glow plug(s). These glow plugs have a distinct marking as per Figure 1 of the TSB Service Procedure below.
concern: Some 2022-2024 Ranger and Everest vehicles equipped with a 3.0L (Lion) diesel engine built from 20 Jun-2023, may exhibit an illuminated Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) with one or more of the following Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs): P066A/P066C/P066E, P067A/P067C/P067E or P06B9/P06BA/P06BB/P06BC/P06BD/P06BE. • The concern has been isolated and only impacts certain glow plugs. The TSB repair procedure has been revised to only replace the affected glow plug(s). These glow plugs have a distinct marking as per Figure 1 of the TSB Service Procedure below.
description: Some 2022-2024 Ranger and Everest vehicles equipped with a 3.0L (Lion) diesel engine built from 20 Jun-2023, may exhibit an illuminated Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) with one or more of the following Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs): P066A/P066C/P066E, P067A/P067C/P067E or P06B9/P06BA/P06BB/P06BC/P06BD/P06BE. • The concern has been isolated and only impacts certain glow plugs. The TSB repair procedure has been revised to only replace the affected glow plug(s). These glow plugs have a distinct marking as per Figure 1 of the TSB Service Procedure below.

title: From The Transmission Area At Light Accelerator Input - 30 June 2025
symptom: Some 2018-2022 Ranger and Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with a 2.0L (Panther) engine may present with concerns of a rattle type noise from the transmission during light to medium accelerator input.
concern: Some 2018-2022 Ranger and Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with a 2.0L (Panther) engine may present with concerns of a rattle type noise from the transmission during light to medium accelerator input.
description: Some 2018-2022 Ranger and Everest/Endeavour vehicles equipped with a 2.0L (Panther) engine may present with concerns of a rattle type noise from the transmission during light to medium accelerator input.

title: Left Hand Rear Leaf Spring Squeak, Scraping Or Knocking Noise - 08 June 2026
symptom: Some 2021-2026 Next-Generation Ranger vehicles equipped with leaf springs, may exhibit squeaking, scraping or knocking noises originating from the Left Hand (LH) rear. This condition may be caused by diesel fuel spilling from the overflow hose during refuelling; the fuel contaminates the leaf spring assembly, resulting in the noise.
concern: Some 2021-2026 Next-Generation Ranger vehicles equipped with leaf springs, may exhibit squeaking, scraping or knocking noises originating from the Left Hand (LH) rear. This condition may be caused by diesel fuel spilling from the overflow hose during refuelling; the fuel contaminates the leaf spring assembly, resulting in the noise.
description: Some 2021-2026 Next-Generation Ranger vehicles equipped with leaf springs, may exhibit squeaking, scraping or knocking noises originating from the Left Hand (LH) rear. title: LED Headlamp Functional Concerns - 10 June 2026
symptom: Some 2021-2026 Next-Generation Ranger and Everest vehicles may experience LED headlamp (LED reflector and LED projector) functionality concerns such as LED Reflector Headlamps (Figure 1) • Day time running lamp inoperable • Low beam inoperable • High beam inoperable LED Projector Headlamps (Figure 2) • Day time running lamp inoperable • Low beam inoperable • High beam inoperable • Turn indicator inoperable
concern: Some 2021-2026 Next-Generation Ranger and Everest vehicles may experience LED headlamp (LED reflector and LED projector) functionality concerns such as LED Reflector Headlamps (Figure 1) • Day time running lamp inoperable • Low beam inoperable • High beam inoperable LED Projector Headlamps (Figure 2) • Day time running lamp inoperable • Low beam inoperable • High beam inoperable • Turn indicator inoperable
